pages.title: QA Testing Guidelines
pages.content:
Our QA process involves three levels of testing:

1. Unit Tests - Written by developers, test individual functions and methods
2. Integration Tests - Test how components work together
3. E2E Tests - Written in Playwright, simulate real user workflows

All tests must pass before merging to main branch. Critical bugs block releases.
pages.author: QA Lead

pages.title: Bug Reporting Process
pages.content:
When reporting bugs in Jira, include:
- Title: Clear description of the issue
- Steps to reproduce: Numbered list of actions
- Expected result: What should happen
- Actual result: What actually happened
- Screenshots: Evidence of the issue
- Environment: Browser, OS, app version

Priority levels: Critical, High, Medium, Low
pages.author: QA Lead

pages.title: Release Checklist
pages.content:
Before each release:
1. All automated tests passing
2. Manual regression testing complete
3. Performance benchmarks met
4. Security scan completed
5. Product sign-off received
6. Release notes drafted

Rollback plan must be documented.
pages.author: Release Manager

pages.title: API Documentation Standards
pages.content:
All APIs must include:
- Endpoint URL and method
- Request/response schemas
- Authentication requirements
- Error codes and messages
- Rate limits

Use OpenAPI 3.0 specification.
pages.author: Tech Lead
